symptom: Customer reports lights getting bright and dim, odd voltages at receptacles, or two circuits sharing a neutral in a panel or junction box.
diagnosis: Possible open neutral on a multiwire branch circuit. Shared-neutral loads can see unstable voltage and damage equipment.
procedure: Tell the user to stop using affected loads until verified.
Turn off both ungrounded conductors serving the shared neutral.
Verify the two breakers have a common trip or handle tie as required.
Meter hot-to-neutral, hot-to-hot, and neutral continuity before reconnecting loads.
Locate the loose neutral at the panel, device, or junction before restoring power.
safety_notes: Do not disconnect a shared neutral under load.
Do not energize only one leg until the neutral is confirmed solid.
Unstable neutral voltage can damage appliances and create shock risk.
